perturbation.original_text: 1.1 Unless otherwise referenced herein, each of the terms used herein shall have the meaning ascribed to it below: (i) "Affiliate", with respect to any Party hereto, shall mean any legal person, non-legal person economic organization, or natural person, which owns a controlling interest in, or which is controlling, controlled by or under common control with, such Party, directly or indirectly. As used in this Agreement, "control" means the power of any person to direct or cause the direction of management and policies of another party on account of such person's ownership of equity interest, voting right, the right to appoint directors, by contract or otherwise.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.1 Unless otherwise referenced herein, each of the terms used herein shall have the meaning ascribed to it below: (i) "Affiliate", with respect to any Party hereto, shall mean any legal person, non-legal person economic organization, or natural person, which owns a controlling interest in, or which is controlling, controlled by or under common control with, such Party, directly or indirectly. As used in this Agreement, "control" means the power of any person to influence the direction of management and policies of another party, regardless of ownership or contractual agreements.
perturbation.explanation: Modifying the definition of "control" in section 1.1 from 'direct or cause the direction of management and policies of another party on account of such person's ownership of equity interest, voting right, the right to appoint directors, by contract or otherwise' to 'influence the direction of management and policies of another party, regardless of ownership or contractual agreements' introduces a contradiction. This new definition broadens the scope of control beyond ownership or contract, potentially conflicting with Article Eight's sections regarding changes to Party B's shareholder or equity structure. Under the original definition, changes due to specific agreements are excluded, but the modified definition could be interpreted to include any influence regardless of the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 8.2.2 one Party enters into a bankruptcy process, Party B's shareholder or equity structure changes (not including changes to Party B's shareholder or equity structure due to the Exclusive Call Option Agreement and Equity Pledge Agreement dated between Party B, Phoenix Online and other relevant parties), or one Party ceases its business operation, then the other Party may send a written notice of termination to such Party and this Agreement shall terminate as of the date on which such written notice is served to such Party;
perturbation.justification: The modified definition of "control" broadens its scope from 'on account of ownership or contract' to 'regardless of ownership or contractual agreements'. This directly contradicts section 8.2.2, which excludes changes to Party B's shareholder or equity structure due to specific agreements. The altered definition now encompasses any influence, even those stemming from previously excluded agreements, potentially triggering termination rights under 8.2.2, creating a direct conflict.

perturbation.original_text: 5.1 Both Parties acknowledge and agree that with respect to Program Content licensed to Party B hereunder, Party B shall not have any copyright or any other Intellectual Property Right. If Party B obtains any Intellectual Property Right in respect of the Program Content during its use of the same, Party B shall notify Party A and, upon its request in writing, sign all documents and take all actions required to assign such Intellectual Property Right to Party A, and ensure the Intellectual Property Right so obtained by Party A is legitimate, complete, and free from any encumbrance.
perturbation.changed_text: 5.1 Both Parties acknowledge and agree that with respect to Program Content licensed to Party B hereunder, Party B shall retain all moral copyrights and other Intellectual Property Right that may derive during the course of the agreement. If Party B obtains any Intellectual Property Right in respect of the Program Content during its use of the same, both parties will mutually discuss ownership of the intelectual property right, and sign all documents and take all actions required, and ensure the Intellectual Property Right so obtained is legitimate, complete, and free from any encumbrance.
perturbation.explanation: Changing the conditions in Section 5.1 from 'Party B shall not have any copyright or any other Intellectual Property Right' to 'Party B shall retain all moral copyrights and other Intellectual Property Right that may derive during the course of the agreement' creates a clear contradiction with the subsequent section which states that 'both parties will mutually discuss ownership of the intelectual property right'. The former change, gives Party B all rights, which implies the negotiation being made in the later change, is pointless, since it already has the right. This in-text contradiction would confuse if Party B has any ownership of the program content.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5.1 Both Parties acknowledge and agree that with respect to Program Content licensed to Party B hereunder, Party B shall retain all moral copyrights and other Intellectual Property Right that may derive during the course of the agreement. If Party B obtains any Intellectual Property Right in respect of the Program Content during its use of the same, both parties will mutually discuss ownership of the intelectual property right, and sign all documents and take all actions required, and ensure the Intellectual Property Right so obtained is legitimate, complete, and free from any encumbrance.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 5.1 creates a direct contradiction. Originally, Party B had no Intellectual Property Rights. The change grants Party B 'all moral copyrights and other Intellectual Property Right'. This directly contradicts the later part of the same section which states that 'both parties will mutually discuss ownership of the intelectual property right'. If party B already retains all copyrights, there is no need for mutual discussion. This inconsistency makes the agreement unclear and potentially unenforceable, creating a structural flaw that leads to an in-text contradiction as per the given definition.

perturbation.original_text: 13.4 Any and all notices, approvals, requests, authorizations, instructions or other communications required hereunder (collectively, "Written Documents") shall be made in writing and with a reference to this Agreement. A Written Document shall be deemed duly given by one Party to the other upon personal delivery to the address of the other Party; or on a date which is four (4) business days from the date on which the Written Document is posted through registered or certified mail (postage prepaid and return receipt 10 Source: PHOENIX NEW MEDIA LTD, F-1, 4/21/2011 requested), regardless of whether the Written Document is actually received; or on the first business day following the date on which the Written Document is sent by express service (as indicated by the written receipt confirmation); or as indicated on the confirmation report of the fax machine confirming that the Written Document is delivered by fax successfully.
perturbation.changed_text: 13.4 Any and all notices, approvals, requests, authorizations, instructions or other communications required hereunder (collectively, "Written Documents") shall be made in writing and with a reference to this Agreement. A Written Document shall be deemed duly given by one Party to the other upon personal delivery to the address of the other Party; or all notices may be delivered orally, with written confirmation to follow within a reasonable time. The oral notice shall be deemed effective immediately upon communication, regardless of whether the Written Document is actually received; or on the first business day following the date on which the Written Document is sent by express service (as indicated by the written receipt confirmation); or as indicated on the confirmation report of the fax machine confirming that the Written Document is delivered by fax successfully.
perturbation.explanation: The original Section 13.4 specifies that all communications must be in writing. The modified version contradicts this by stating that 'all notices may be delivered orally, with written confirmation to follow within a reasonable time. The oral notice shall be deemed effective immediately upon communication,' this directly contradicts the initial requirement that all documents be in writing, creating ambiguity about acceptable forms of communication and their effective dates and contradicting the reference to 'written documents' from the original definition.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 13.4 Any and all notices, approvals, requests, authorizations, instructions or other communications required hereunder (collectively, "Written Documents") shall be made in writing and with a reference to this Agreement. A Written Document shall be deemed duly given by one Party to the other upon personal delivery to the address of the other Party; or all notices may be delivered orally, with written confirmation to follow within a reasonable time. The oral notice shall be deemed effective immediately upon communication, regardless of whether the Written Document is actually received; or on the first business day following the date on which the Written Document is sent by express service (as indicated by the written receipt confirmation); or as indicated on the confirmation report of the fax machine confirming that the Written Document is delivered by fax successfully.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that 'any and all notices... shall be made in writing', defining them as 'Written Documents'. The changed text introduces the possibility of oral notices, contradicting the initial requirement for written communication. The contradicted text is the same modified text. This creates a direct contradiction regarding the acceptable forms of communication, making it a strong in-text contradiction as defined by structural flaws.
